tldr: The Andes hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius (8 confirmed cases, 3 dead, 11 total) is generating three distinct framings globally: outbreak containment and quarantine, border security and airport screening, and COVID-era misinformation recycling (ivermectin). Indonesia is the most isolated bucket (0.350), covering the story entirely through local airport supervision.
event_summary: Eight passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship tested positive for Andes hantavirus; three have died and six passengers arrived in Australia for a three-week quarantine. The WHO confirmed the outbreak.

evidence.quote: Eight people infected in hantavirus outbreak aboard MV Hondius cruise ship have tested positive for Andes virus; 'No indication' strain of hantavirus has mutated, says EU agency.
Six passengers from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak arrived Friday in Australia for a quarantine expected to last at least three weeks.
Banten tightens supervision at Soetta Airport to prevent hantavirus
Vietnam and Cambodia have taken preventive measures against hantavirus disease over a recent cruise ship outbreak, including health monitoring of passengers entering international airports.
Those who cheered ivermectin as a Covid-19 treatment are now making unsubstantiated claims about its use against hantavirus.
many people needlessly fretting about a return to lockdowns, social distancing and masks.
He was somewhere in the South Atlantic when a friend texted him about an outbreak on a cruise: “Please tell me you’re not on this ship.”
Welcome to hantavirus quarantine: Six people including a Brit arrive at remote Australian facility where they will be kept in isolation for at least three weeks
"En cas de pandémie, nous ne sommes pas prêts" : en Argentine, la recherche sur l'hantavirus tente d'avancer malgré les coupes budgétaires

isolation_top.note: Covers story entirely through local airport border-security lens — Banten province and Soekarno-Hatta Airport supervision — diverging from the outbreak/quarantine framing dominant across all other buckets.

German-language DW articles frame story through epidemic-classification terminology (Endemie/Epidemie/Pandemie) and explicitly compare to COVID — both a language artefact and a distinctive conceptual angle.
The Intercept frames the story through COVID-era misinformation recycling (ivermectin super fans), diverging entirely from the medical/quarantine coverage dominant elsewhere.
New Yorker runs a first-person narrative by a scientist who was aboard the Hondius — diverges from third-person outbreak coverage.
O Globo covers the story through a quarantine diarist (Jake Rosmarin) and his Starbucks orders in Nebraska — Portuguese-language artefact plus entirely personal narrative angle.

exclusive_vocab_highlights.what_it_reveals: The Intercept uniquely frames the outbreak as a COVID-misinformation revival event — vocabulary that signals a media-criticism story, not an outbreak story.
Banten is the province, Soetta is the Soekarno-Hatta Airport abbreviation — purely local geographic identifiers that confirm indonesia is covering its own border response, not the global outbreak.

French-language artefact from France Info on Argentine research funding cuts under Javier Milei — editorially significant because it links the outbreak to a scientific capacity story absent from all other buckets.

single_outlet_findings.finding: The only US outlet in the corpus frames the outbreak as a COVID-misinformation recurrence story (ivermectin super fans), making no mention of the quarantine logistics or case counts that dominate all other buckets.

The only outlet to connect the outbreak to Argentina's gutted scientific research capacity under Milei's antivax government — a structural story about epidemic preparedness that the rest of the corpus ignores.
A first-person narrative by a scientist aboard the MV Hondius — the only first-person account in the corpus, covering the outbreak from inside the ship.

bottom_line: The Hondius outbreak generated three globally distinct framings: (1) epidemiological/quarantine tracking (dominant), (2) border-security containment (Indonesia, Vietnam, North Korea), and (3) COVID-era misinformation recycling (USA, India). Indonesia's extreme isolation (0.350) traces to covering its local airport rather than the outbreak. Wire_services includes both an AP English quarantine report and a French-language capacity piece on Argentine research funding — the latter connecting outbreak readiness to Milei’s antivax funding cuts, an angle invisible to the rest of the corpus.
